folio.l: ence , put a wey sorwe out of ©oure herte

¢ Remembre ©ow
that Iúc Syrak seitú , a man that is ioyous in herte and
glad , it hi~ co~seruytú florisshyngö in his age , but sootúly , sor-
we
in herte sleetú ful many a man

¢ Salamon seitú , that
rigút as motúthes in the shepes flees , a noyen to the clothes ,
and the smale wormes to the tree ; rigút so anoyetú sorwe
to the herte

¢ wherfore vs ougúte as wel in the detú of oure
children , as in the losse of oure goodes temporeles , haue pacien-
ce

¢ Remembre ©ow vp on the pacient Iob , whan he hadde lost
his children , And his temporel substaunce , and in his body en-
dured and receyued ful many a greuous tébulaciou~ , ©et seide he
thus ,

Oure lord hatú ©eue it me , Oure lord hatú biraft it me ,
rigút as oure lord hatú wold , rigút so is it don , I blissed be the
name of oure lord

¢ To theise forseide thyngis answered Melibe-
us vn to his wyf ¢ Prudence , Alle thyne wordes q¶ he ben sothe
and æä to ¸fitable , but trewely myn herte is troubled , witú this
sorwe so greuously , that I not what to done

¢ lat calle q¶ Pèdence
thyne trewe frendes alle , and thy lynage , whicú that ben wyse ,
telletú ©oure caas , and herkenetú what they seye in counseilly~ge ,
And ©ow gouäne after here sentence

¢ Salamon seitú , werke aÂ
thynges by counseille , and æè shalt neuäe repente

¢ Tha~ne by
the counseil of his wyf Prudence ; this Melibeus , let callen a gret
co~gregaciou~ of folk ,

as Surgiens , Phisiciens , olde folk and ©onge ,
And some of hise olde enemyes reconsiled , as bi here semblaunt ,
to his loue , and in to his grace ,

and ther witú is witú on an erasure?/ODR/ al , ther comen
so~me of his neygúbores , that diden hi~ reuäence , more for drede
than for loue , as it happetú ofte

¢ Ther comen also ful many
subtile flat äerers . and wyse aduocatz lerned in the lawe ,

And wha~
this folk togydre assembled weren , This Melibeus in sorweful .
wyse , shewed he~ his cas ,

And by the manere of his speche , it
semed that in herte he bar a crewel Ire , redy to don vengeau~ce
vp oon his foos , & sodeynly desired , that the werre shulde bigy~ne ,
but nathelees , ©et axid he his cou~seil vp oon this matere ,

A sur-
gien by licence and assent of swicú as weren wyse , vp roos
And vn to Melibeus , seide , as ©e moun heere ,

Sire q¶ he , as to
vs Surgiens ap¹tenetú , that we do to euäy wygút , the beste that
we kan , where as we ben witú holde , and to oure pacientz , that
we do no damage ,

wherfore it happetú many tyme and ofte ,
that whan twey men han euäycú wounded other , O same sur-
gien heletú he~ bothe ,

wherfore vn to oure art , it is nat ¹tine~t
to norice werre , ne ¹ties to supporte ,

but certes as to the wa-
risshynge of ©oure dougúter , al be it so , that she ¹ilously be
